page: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64 and supports instruction set extensions including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per vCPU.

This instance includes 3686 GB of local NVMe storage and is premium storage capable. Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with high marks in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Passmark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, showing strong throughput in both small-block cached and large-block uncached read/write operations.

While multi-core and database operations (such as Redis SET and Passmark database tests) perform in the top tier, single-core performance remains in the average category. This performance profile makes the instance highly effective for concurrent workloads, static web serving, and database management, but less optimal for single-threaded tasks. Additionally, while the server handles prompt processing for small LLM models efficiently, text generation for large 70B parameter models is a notable bottleneck, falling into the bottom performance tier due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv5 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It provides 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 2.8 GHz, 384.0 GB of RAM, and 3686 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core CPU capabilities, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database operation throughput, though single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation remain average to weak. This resource profile offers balanced cost efficiency for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database hosting, and high-throughput static web serving, while avoiding the premium cost of specialized GPU hardware.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v5 is a DDSv5 virtual machine featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v5 is a DDSv5 virtual machine with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity virtual machine balancing ninety-six dedicated Intel vCPUs, local NVMe storage, and strong multi-threaded performance for enterprise databases.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C vCPUs and 384 GB RAM
3686 GB of local NVMe storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Average single-core performance and weak large-model LLM text generation
Well-suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, databases, and web serving
